storyInfo: One morning, Amiya falls into dream in search of her companions. A shadow follows her from afar. Will she appear before Amiya without being summoned?

attributes.text: \nExecutors: Dur-nar, Rosa, Zima, Istina, Gummy, Leto
\n_ESTABLISHING CROSS-VERIFICATION LINK WITH NODE [DWDB-221E] (AUTHORIZATION CODE: CE)

attributes.content: Phew...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: What time is it...?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Did I oversleep...?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I need to hurry... Huh? What... am I in a hurry for?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya comes to her senses. There is a knock at the door, by a gentle hand...
Ah, I'll be right there!
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The corridor's... gone?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Pushing the door open, Amiya steps into the wind.
Summer has only just arrived. An azure sky hangs low, blanketed by thick piles of white clouds.
She steps into an early summer meadow, bent grass blades scratching her feet as dew slides down the creases of stems and leaves, spreading the scent of grass into the air.
Good morning. You finally woke up.
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: ...Good morning?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Now that's out of the way, it's time for the important stuff—
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: Did you find them yet?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: Why did you sleep in then...? You lost them all. Aren't you worried?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: I-I'm heading out to look for them right now!
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: ...Do you know where I lost them?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I should be asking you that! Aren't you always with them?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: If you don't know where they went, if you don't go looking for them, who do you think is going to find them?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: You're right, I'm the one who'd know where they went...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Where... did we part ways?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: When I saw you guys lying on that big snoring burdenbeast in the wastes, did you lose them then?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: Burdenbeast... No, that wasn't a burdenbeast.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: You mean the mountain, right?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The child looks in the direction Amiya is pointing. At the end of the plains sits a colossal mountain under the transparent sunlight.
In a blink, the sun seems to solidify, as if the entire mountain has been encased in a giant amber.
Amiya closes her eyes in disbelief, and opens them again to see the mountain back to normal.
Did it ever snore? Amiya has doubts. It seems so silent right now.
It wasn't a burdenbeast...? Um, anyways, if you think that's the spot, then that's where you should be headed.
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: You should go now, right? Who knows, they might have been waiting long for you, and you shouldn't keep them waiting... besides, you don't have much time, do you?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: ...You're right.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya politely thanks the child for waking her up.
Then, she walks through the early summer breeze, toward the giant mountain.
Behind Amiya, the wind coils into an open cabin.
A shadow quietly stands next to the narrow bed, looking at Amiya and the child past the door.
The shadow listens to their conversation, to their farewell, and watches Amiya walk towards the giant mountain.
After leaving the cabin, Amiya never turns back.
And so she never sees the shadow.
...Why are you so hard on yourself?
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: Hard...? Me?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: I'm just... a little anxious.
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: If you're really tired, it's fine to rest for a while longer.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: But 'I' lost everyone... they might be waiting for 'me', they might have been waiting for a long time...
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: Everyone believes in 'me'... so 'I' have to find them quick... or else...
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: Or else what?
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: ...The fields are so vast, the mountain so high. Getting an early start... is the first thing 'I' can do.
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: Yes. You'll do many more things on your journey. So it's fine to take it slow.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: Will this road... be a really long one?
attributes.name: Child

attributes.content: I'll always be with you.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: No matter how long it is, I'll be with you until the end.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: The young illusion cradles the shadow's neck with her arms, and dissipates into the grassy wind without a word.
The shadow gets to her feet, and turns toward the little figure trudging toward the mountain.
The giant mountain peak is flat and wide. From it one can see yet another summit.
This is where I last saw them, right...?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya circles the mountain peak in search of traces or clues... will she find their footprints? Did they leave a sign within the shadows cast by the rocks?
But the flat and wide peak bares all that it has. Other than the sunlight, wind, and dust kicked up by her steps, there is nothing else to be found.
...*sigh* Where can I find some clues...?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Why didn't they tell me where exactly they were going...?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: She looks at her own toes as her heart seems to be weighed down by something. She wants to breathe out, but it seems like her lungs don't have enough air...
And then...
A small black thing jumps up from a crack in the precipice, dropping into Amiya's arms, and scares a shriek out of her!
Don't be alarmed.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Sorry... I didn't think I'd find a cloudbeast here.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I heard your question. Perhaps you missed it, but they did tell you.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: As far as I know, they sent you a letter.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: A letter?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya holds the cloudbeast with her left hand, letting it calmly sit on her arm as she rifles through her pockets with her right hand.
Left... right... she feels something soft, her fingertips touch something damp.
So there really was a letter, right in my pocket... I'm glad it didn't fall out.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: She cautiously pats her right pocket. She has worn this jacket for a long time.
You shouldn't stuff your pockets so much.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Read it. Perhaps you might find the information you need, where they might have gone.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: But... I can't read a single word...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya holds the cloudbeast's fluffy body with her left hand and together with her right, tries her best to straighten the soggy paper.
It's thoroughly soaked.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I see. You couldn't find them because the letter was damaged, and you couldn't read the information within.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Who did this?
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Amiya lowers her head, unsure how to answer the cloudbeast...
Because she was the one who drenched it...
I... couldn't help it. The water came out, just like that.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: It kept flowing from my face, and no matter how I tried to stop it, I couldn't... in the end, the whole letter was soaked.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The cloudbeast lets out a somewhat astonished 'hm' upon hearing Amiya's answer, and jumps onto Amiya's shoulder.

As if conducting a physical examination, the cloudbeast carefully examines Amiya's face, even raising its front paw to cautiously touch her cheek in different spots. The feeling is somewhat familiar... like a doctor's touch.
This isn't right.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Water should not be seeping out from puppet faces.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: We need to consider the possibility of malfunction, or even failure of bodily functions... and figure out a solution.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Amiya also touches her own cheek and shakes her head.
I don't know...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The cloudbeast jumps up to Amiya's head, contemplates for a moment, then jumps back into Amiya's arms.
It looks like a creature deep in thought.
I understand. You should look for the magician.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: The magician... oh yes! The magician can do anything!
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Good. Let the magician fix you, so water no longer seeps through. This way, the letter won't be soaked and unreadable.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Once you can read the information written on it, you'll know where they went.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: If you say so... I will!
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: ...But where do I find the magician?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I walked through the whole field, climbed to the top of the giant mountain, but I haven't seen anyone else other than the kid who woke me up, and you...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Is that so? That is somewhat strange... When you meet the magician, ask for an eye exam too.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: But I have to find the magician first...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: You can find the magician. You're the only one who can.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Could you at least... tell me which direction I should head in?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The cloudbeast lowers its eyes, as if from exhaustion. Amiya notices it trying to stifle a yawn.
You need to go at once...
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: The cloudbeast shakes its head as if to stave off its drowsiness. It leaps once more onto Amiya's head—
Amiya feels a gentle kick on her ear, and feels the weight vanish as the cloudbeast leaps into the fading light, disappearing into approaching dusk.
Amiya looks up, trying to find the creature, but the open and flat mountaintop is completely deserted.
She puts the letter back into her jacket. After patting her pocket, Amiya makes up her mind and closes her eyes.
She is a little nervous, but fortunately, she can do it.
Like the leaping cloudbeast earlier, she runs a ways, then jumps off the edge of the cliff—
Amiya's long ponytail draws arcs in the air as she runs, captured in the shadow's eyes.
Amiya leaps off the cliff, into the formless air.
The shadow raises her pale face.
Amiya does not turn back, and the shadow remains standing, far behind her.
The shadow sighs softly, recalling the earlier conversation between Amiya and the cloudbeast.
She reaches out, and the cloudbeast emerges from thin air, standing on the shadow's palm.
I have done what I had to do.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: ...Is that what you think?
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: She needed a guide. That has always been 'my' responsibility.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: If we were talking about your true self... I actually think you should have given her something else.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: 'I' shouldn't be the one to grant her those things.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Why not? She's always been so dependent on 'you'...
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: My point is, 'you' are more suited to the role than I am.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: You know she and I made a promise. I'll appear whenever she calls for me.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: She knows I'm always there, but she's been calling for me less and less the past few years.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: She's no longer a child.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: The shadow recalls when they first met—oh, sorry, the shadow always gets it wrong. 'When they first met' actually happened a little later.
Amiya was once a girl who would snuggle up to her, burying a few tears into the fabric of her shoulder. She would then raise her head to look at the shadow's eyes, and smile.
The shadow spent some time learning these smiles, so she could use them too.
But time passed faster than she could perceive it, something she originally found hard to grasp.
Though, it's not as if she doesn't know why Amiya no longer calls for her.
Amiya is growing up, the shadow thinks.
Actually, it isn't so much about how 'you' think, but what she wants, no?
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: Of course. After all, 'I' am one of the people she's looking for.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: So why would she seek solace from 'me'? 'I'... have become one of her responsibilities too.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: She will not allow herself to remove her 'emotions' from her 'responsibilities'.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: ...*sigh*.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: Though, perhaps that one can.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Are you going to follow her? Do you... want to meet the magician?
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: ...I'll accompany her. It is the reason I exist in this form.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: If she's willing to ask the magician for help, I won't have to break our promise and show up without her summoning me.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: If not... I shall decide then whether or not to fulfill my obligations.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: I thought your 'promise' and 'obligation' were one and the same?
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: Logically speaking, yes.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: ...Alright. I'll leave it to you.
attributes.name: Cloudbeast

attributes.content: The shadow and the cloudbeast bid each other goodbye. Their conversation lasted longer than she expected.
As twilight draws near, she has to catch up with Amiya as soon as she can.
Amiya leaps off the cliff, but does not fall. She rises into the sky, stepping on air and sparks of dusk, searching for the magician among the stars and clouds.
Strange. The magician isn't among the stars.
Amiya jumps onto a cloud, then another, asking them to move aside so she can see what lies behind, then asks them to congregate, becoming a soft bridge.
Amiya has always been a patient girl, and never fears disappointment, so she manages to find the magician in the end.
Hello, great magician. There's something I need urgently... Could you please fix me?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I lost people very important to me, and I have to find them as soon as I can.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: But I don't know why, my face keeps leaking water, soaking the letter they gave me, so I can't read a single word...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Why would water even leak from a puppet's face? The cloudbeast is right, maybe I'm broken...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: O great magician, could you please fix me up quickly? I'm really in a hurry... I have to read what's on the letter and find them.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya, you're not broken. You're just crying...
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: Tears don't mean you're damaged inside.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: So a puppet... can cry too?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The river of tears is already long enough...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: It's alright, Amiya. It's alright. Everyone cries. We can't stop the river of tears from flowing even if we cover our eyes and faces. If the river dries up, the heart does too.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: If your heart shrivels up, there won't be space for those dear to you to fit in, right?
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: The magician wipes Amiya's cheek, and the spot turns damp again.
Amiya, you can't see it, but your tears are shining... Oh, maybe because the stars are out, and their light is reflecting on your face.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: Trust me, Amiya.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: I do... I always have and will. But, the letter...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I'll help you. This won't be hard.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: The magician summons ten clouds, enveloping the two. Bright red clouds like the sunset that feel warm, even a little hot to the touch.
The magician asks Amiya to unfold the letter, hold it in her fingers, and hang it from a cloud.
In just a short while, the fiery cloud's shade of red fades into a grey and turns damp.
The fiery cloud nods to the magician—never mind where its head even is, it's what the magician says—and strolls into the troposphere.
This happens ten times, and Amiya picks up the letter, now dry and firm, no longer damp and flimsy.
The letter's words stand up straight in a neat line, every stroke now rendered clear.
Amiya takes a quick glance.
Phew...
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: Having done all of that, the magician droops, settling onto a thick cloud.
Are you alright? You seem exhausted...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Calling these clouds out and making them proper is no easy feat... But I'll be fine after a brief rest.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: So, what does the letter say? Where did they go? I can look for them with you.
attributes.name: Magician

attributes.content: Amiya is pondering on how to answer the magician when she sees sparkles on the other's face... like starlight falling across the magician's visage.
She suddenly recalls herself rushing the magician for help, focusing on her own troubles and worries, asking the magician to dry the letter with magic. But clearly...
They are important to the magician as well.
Before she arrived, was the magician... crying too?
Immediately, an overwhelming sense of guilt rushes into her heart, enveloping her like a giant bubble.
Amiya kneels within it, fists pounding on the transparent membrane. It seems so fragile and thin with its swirling liquid, yet she fails to pop it no matter what she does.
Amiya sees the magician's lips move, as if saying something to her of urgency—
But the bubble quickly wobbles away.
With the letter still in Amiya's hand.
The shadow arrives somewhat late, coming just as the bubble floats away.
If, the shadow thinks, she was a little earlier, could she have gotten to talk to the magician?
She looks at the figure by the stars. The magician is already standing, seemingly wanting to grab the bubble, but it merely slips through the magician's fingertips.
The shadow subconsciously smiles.
The magician looks up and sees her.
It's time to catch up with the floating bubble. The shadow shakes her head at the magician.
The magician blinks, and nods to the shadow.
How unfortunate her slight lateness leaves them no time to exchange even a few words.
But fortunately, there is still just enough time for them to trade glances.
Beyond the clouds and stars, the magician watches the shadow disappear into the distance.
The bubble with Amiya inside drifts toward the desert.
Perhaps the dry air finally evaporates all moisture from the bubble, for it bursts with a 'pop'.
Hot air endlessly rises from the desert surface, bumping Amiya down to the ground.
Amiya tries to stand. She grabs the sand, but the heat makes her pull back. She then uses her sleeve as padding, which helps a little.
She reads the letter again—

attributes.text: 'Amiya, I wish you a smooth journey.'

\n\n'If these words ever reach you... Amiya, you've shouldered all of this for the Sarkaz, and I feel ashamed whenever I think of it. I can't take you away from the battlefield, and it may sound irresponsible, but Amiya, I hope you will treasure yourself. Don't forget, no matter how cruel your battlefield can be.'

\n\n'Amiya, you told me before I don't misjudge people, so please take my advice: Save some of your own gentleness for yourself, forget your burdens on occasion, and indulge a sweet treat every now and then. You have a long journey ahead of you.'
\n\n'I will protect all of you until the end.'

attributes.content: The letter does not mention where they went, or perhaps there is no need to say.
The letter only contains many requests, and farewells.
Amiya holds them in her hands, looking to the horizon—does the end of the desert lie on the other side?
I have to go.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Even if I can't find them... I have to find my own way out. There's so much to do... there are so many people that need me.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya walks.
The sun cooks her back, casting a shadow before her. Time passes, but the sunlight does not fade as the blazing sun hangs high, never setting.
The bright light casts a shadow with knife-sharp, jagged edges. Amiya's every step forward treads on the boundary between black shadow and white sand.
Amiya walks.
The sun scorches her neck as sweat rolls down her brows and into her eyes, as sweat on her forehead evaporates into dry salt crystals.
She cannot tell if her distorted vision is caused by the heat waves or the stinging in her eyes.
Amiya walks.
Sudden gusts of wind throw sand and gravel into her face, while breathing dry air feels like inhaling blades slicing into her windpipe.
She tries to cover her mouth to retain any shred of moisture from her breaths, and her lips touch the paper in her hand.
Amiya walks.
She seems unable to stand upright any longer, but even so she trudges onward, knees bent.

Pulling her legs from quicksand is like drawing a rusty sword from its scabbard. Was the sensation under her feet actually coming from her own body? Thoughts like these drift through her hazy mind like wisps of cotton.
The shadow remains far behind Amiya.
She can see further than Amiya can. She sees what lies far away in front of Amiya, the desert edge receding with the young Cautus's steps, always a step away from the horizon.
The shadow notices Amiya's eyes are almost shut, but she trudges on. Despite this, she remains in the middle of the desert.
Until Amiya allows herself to exit, the desert will go on forever.
The black crown's master is prone to dreams. Many dreams do not come from themselves, but the shadow can tell them apart, and knows Amiya is the master of this dreamland.
The shadow pauses. She makes up her mind.
She has not done this in a long time, but neither has she seen Amiya like this in a long time.
Amiya looks up, and a color she hasn't seen for a long time flashes in the corner of her eye.
A verdant shade. Even the wind blowing past her face holds the tiniest bit of moisture.
...An oasis.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: But there shouldn't be an oasis here...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: ...No, I can't, walk to the mirage.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya turns around, avoiding the oasis.
And a strange thing happens...
The oasis lifts itself up. From the bottom—what bottom, exactly?—four legs stretch out, like the feet of a wooden wardrobe.
A wardrobe's feet will never be as flexible as these ones, and the legs move left and right as it rotates its, uh, 'ankles' to warm up...
Then it starts running, and lands with a 'thump' right before Amiya.
Her eyes widen.
She tries to move a few steps to the right, and the oasis lifts itself up to follow suit.
Amiya moves slightly to the left, and the oasis once again stretches its legs...
Amiya cautiously takes a step back—
The oasis picks itself up, and charges straight at Amiya!
It then steadily lands itself before her.
The teal lake ripples in gentle waves, swaying Amiya's reflection in the water.
Finally, in this dream, she turns around for the first time to look behind her, but finds no one, only the wind and sand from the path behind her.
...Is that you?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: There is no answer.
But as Amiya turns around, the shadow is already in front of her, as if she was always there, with the same smile in Amiya's memories.
Amiya.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: The serene expression overlaps with a smile Amiya remembers.
She is momentarily speechless.
The shadow also sees Amiya's memories, and so, holds Amiya in a gentle embrace, just as the memories play out.
...Theresa...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The shadow gently pats the girl's back as if in response.
I couldn't... I didn't stop it... it's happened so many times...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Dr. Kal'tsit... made so many preparations...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: But I couldn't bring them back... there are so many... important people...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: ...What should I do...? I know I'm not the only one in pain... but I have to rely on...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Everyone needs... a shoulder to lean on now. But I carry everyone's trust on my shoulders... so how can I wallow in sorrow...?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: I just have... too little... time...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: ...Had I the time myself, I wanted to tell you...
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: Not everything can be brought back. To 'bear' a burden... also means accepting the irreversible.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: But I know you're just really hurt.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: You just wish you could've done something... I know how it feels.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: But I also... feel awful.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: Amiya's tears fall as they always have, but this time, they no longer soak fabric; instead, they slide into the gap between shadow and dream.
Water droplets fall through the shadow, and dissipate like mist before touching Amiya's fingertips.
Amiya looks up at the shadow's face, tears welling up in her eyes. A sadness that stirs up a different sort of sorrow.
Amiya is no longer a child.
She was too big to lie in the shadow's lap a long time ago.
People always say Amiya is a precocious child, taking on responsibilities far beyond her age. But Amiya knows, she herself has indulged in the privileges of being a child.
For example, once within a nightmare, she held onto the shadow's fingers while feigning ignorance, telling her the trivialities she already knew.
Or like now, as she presses her forehead against something warm, confiding in the familiar.
Even though she is long dead and gone.
The shadow smiles at her, like an echo.
Amiya closes her eyes, new tears spilling out, rolling down her cheeks. She suddenly feels an unbearable sorrow...
...Theresa... please, you no longer have to...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The shadow reaches out and touches Amiya's forehead.
They made a promise. The shadow will appear whenever Amiya calls for her.
Amiya needs only to refuse the shadow, and she will disappear.
Unspoken words linger in the air.
But the shadow remains standing.
She leans down, and leaves a somewhat strained kiss on Amiya's head, then stands up and waits.
But Amiya comes to her senses, and her vulnerable state leaves her open to making another mistake.
How could I have said that...? I'm sorry, Theresa...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: The shadow stops smiling, and Amiya's sorrowful expression appears on her face.
She hugs Amiya again, her cheek pressing against Amiya's temple. This way, Amiya cannot see the sadness on her face.
As she gently pats the Cautus's back, she says...
Amiya... don't worry about me.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: You will never hurt me. You never have to apologize to me.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: I know you're growing up. It's alright. It's good.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: I'm always here.
attributes.name: Shadow

attributes.content: As she finishes, the shadow releases Amiya from her embrace, squeezes her hand, and vanishes.
The endless stretch of sand disappears without a trace, and in the void within the void, the teal lake ripples slowly, the moist wind still tender.
Amiya opens her eyes. They sting a little. Her hair and pillow are wet.
Someone is holding her hand.
Doctor...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: It's so dark outside... is it morning already?
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.options: Yes. Seems like it'll rain today.

attributes.content: Ah, over there in the forest...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: It's fine, let's set off before the rain falls.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.options: Are you alright, Amiya?;You had a nightmare...

attributes.content: I did... have a really exhausting dream.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: But it had a happy ending.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Because someone was with me. We went through the dream together.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Mm...
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: So, Doctor, I'll go with you too.
attributes.name: Amiya

attributes.content: Amiya wipes the moisture from her eyes, removing any traces of water, and smiles at you.

attributes.text: I broke our 'promise' to fulfill my 'obligation', even though these two are logically the same, and there should not be a situation in which one is affirmed while the other is nullified.
\n\nBut it did happen. I checked the progress logs, but found no errors.
\n\nAdditionally, I already 'believed' this was the right choice before the verification happened.
\n\nJust like the request Amiya made to me.
\n\nI also had a 'premonition' of something I long knew: Amiya will grow, and change.
One question: Will there be a day when she 'will no longer need my company'?
\n\nAnother question: Logically speaking, Amiya will cease to exist in the foreseeable future. In this situation, what should 'I' do?
Strictly speaking, Civilight Eterna grows the same way as time.
\n\nBut the more I learn about Amiya, the more I run into events that show I don't understand 'me' at all.
\n\nHowever, just as when I started to 'believe', contradictory circumstances also became valid.
\n\nI also 'believe' the question of 'what should I do' is a topic not worth worrying about.
\n\nIt may be far from inception, but Civilight Eterna was defined to be a management device for all memories.
But now, I'm beginning to understand what 'memories' are.
\n\nThat problem is not worth worrying over, because I have learned I too have memories.
\n\nEven if they are memories of sorrow.
\n\nOr perhaps they are memories of love.
